items.review_definition_single_state_ic: A single-state reward functional $R$ is incentive compatible exactly when $\mathrm{singleStateMeasurableIncentiveCompatible}(R)$ holds.
items.review_definition_dynamic_ic: A dynamic reward functional $R$ is dynamically incentive compatible exactly when $\mathrm{dynamicIncentiveCompatible}(R)$ holds.
items.review_definition_threshold_policy: For a pricing function $w$, cutoff $c$, and policy $\sigma$, the threshold-policy condition is $\mathrm{thresholdRatePolicy}(w,c,\sigma)$.
items.review_definition_dynamic_defined_reward: For two trip-length measures $\mu: \mathrm{Fin}\,2 \to \mathrm{Measure}(\mathrm{TripLength})$, the associated defined-reward data form the type $\mathrm{DynamicDefinedReward}(\mu)$.
items.review_section2_single_state_renewal_rew: In any measurable IID single-state renewal-cycle model $C$, the empirical average payment per empirical average cycle time converges almost surely, as $n\to\infty$, to $\mathrm{singleStateRenewalReward}(\mu,\mathrm{arrivalRate},w,\sigma)$.
items.review_proposition3_1_affine_single_stat: For every trip-length measure $\mu$ satisfying the single-state assumptions, arrival rate $\lambda>0$, and parameters $m,a$ with $0\le a\le m/\lambda$, the affine single-state renewal reward $\mathrm{affineSingleStateRenewalReward}(\mu,\lambda,m,a)$ is single-state measurable incentive compatible.
items.review_theorem1_single_state_threshold_b: If $w(\tau)/\tau$ is measurable and nonnegative on positive trip lengths, the accept-all policy has finite nonzero mass and integrable payment/time, the arrival rate is positive, and accept-all gives positive trip payment, then there are $c\ge0$ and a policy $\sigma$ such that $\sigma$ is a threshold-rate policy for $(w,c)$ and is optimal for $\mathrm{singleStateRenewalReward}(\mu,\lambda,w)$.
items.review_lemma4_single_state_threshold_uni: Under the same single-state measurability, integrability, positivity, and accept-all assumptions, there are $c^\ast\ge0$ and $\sigma^\ast$ such that $\sigma^\ast$ is a threshold-rate policy, has renewal reward $c^\ast$, and is optimal; moreover every measurable optimal $\rho\subseteq\mathrm{acceptAllPolicy}$ differs from the strict/complete threshold bands at $c^\ast$ only on sets of zero single-state trip mass.
items.review_lemma1_measured_dynamic_reward_de: For any two state measures, arrival rates, switching rates, pricing functions, and policies, the measured dynamic reward equals the time fraction in state $I$ times its measured state reward rate plus the corresponding time fraction in state $J$ times its measured state reward rate.
items.review_lemma3_measured_time_fraction_for: If both state cycle denominators $\lambda_I\,\mathrm{mass}_{\mu_I}(\sigma_I)+q_{IJ}$ and $\lambda_J\,\mathrm{mass}_{\mu_J}(\sigma_J)+q_{JI}$ are nonzero, then the cycle-based time-fraction expression built from cross-subcycle probabilities and subcycle lengths equals $\mathrm{gn21MeasuredTimeFraction}(\mu_I,\mu_J,\lambda_I,\lambda_J,q_{IJ},q_{JI},\sigma_I,\sigma_J)$.
items.review_remark1_switch_probability_per_ti: If $\lambda_{IJ}>0$ and $\lambda_{IJ}+\lambda_{JI}>0$, then $u\mapsto \mathrm{gn21SwitchProb}(\lambda_{IJ},\lambda_{JI},u)/u$ is strictly decreasing on $(0,\infty)$.
items.review_lemma5_fixed_response_policy_form: Let $\mu$ be nonatomic, let a measurable integrable response function satisfy the Lemma 5 positive-response shape data, and let a measurable policy $\sigma\subseteq\mathrm{acceptAllPolicy}$ maximize the Lemma 5 marginal set reward among measurable subsets of accept-all. Then $\sigma$ has the corresponding Lemma 5 policy form almost everywhere with respect to $\mu$.
items.review_lemma6_upper_endpoint_derivative_: Under the stated positivity, algebraic identification, nonzero-denominator, interval-integrability, strong-measurability, and continuity assumptions, the endpoint aggregate dynamic reward has the displayed derivative at $u$; that derivative has the same strict sign as the Lemma 6 response evaluated at the endpoint data.
items.review_lemma7_affine_positive_additive_r: If $m>0$, $a>0$, $R_j-R_i\le0$, $Q_i/T_i+Q_j/T_j>0$, $\lambda_{IJ}>0$, $\lambda_{IJ}+\lambda_{JI}>0$, and $T_i,T_j\ne0$, then the Lemma 6 response with switch probability $\mathrm{gn21SwitchProb}(\lambda_{IJ},\lambda_{JI},u)$ and payment argument $m\,u+a$ is strictly quasi-convex on positive $u$.
items.review_lemma8_affine_negative_additive_r: If $m>0$, $a<0$, $R_j-R_i\ge0$, $Q_i/T_i+Q_j/T_j>0$, $\lambda_{IJ}>0$, $\lambda_{IJ}+\lambda_{JI}>0$, and $T_i,T_j\ne0$, then the Lemma 6 response with switch probability $\mathrm{gn21SwitchProb}(\lambda_{IJ},\lambda_{JI},u)$ and payment argument $m\,u+a$ is strictly quasi-concave on positive $u$.
items.review_lemma9_surge_derivative_positive_: For a Lemma 6 derivative certificate $C$ whose fields match the surge-state endpoint, payment, queue, time, and reward identities, if the structured surge bounds, ratio identity $z=\mathrm{ratio}(m-R_1)$, positivity/nonnegativity assumptions, switch-probability assumptions, and current-bound inequalities all hold, then $C.\mathrm{derivativeValue}>0$.
items.review_lemma10_nonsurge_derivative_posit: For a Lemma 6 derivative certificate $C$ whose fields match the non-surge-state endpoint, payment, queue, time, and reward identities, if the structured non-surge bounds, ratio identity $z=\mathrm{ratio}\,R_2$, positivity assumptions, switch-probability assumptions, and current-bound inequalities all hold, then $C.\mathrm{derivativeValue}>0$.
items.review_theorem2_multiplicative_policy_sh: Given two state measures, a dynamic reward $R$, and all-measurable feasible almost-everywhere policy-form data, assume every optimal policy has non-surge derivative shape positive or strictly decreasing and surge derivative shape positive or strictly increasing. Then some optimal policy rejects long trips in state $0$ up to a finite-or-infinite cutoff almost everywhere and rejects short trips in state $1$ almost everywhere, and every optimal policy has those same two almost-everywhere shape properties.
items.review_theorem2_multiplicative_positive_: For the explicit atomic multiplicative-pricing instance with switch rates $1/4$ and $1/4$, there is a positive cutoff $2$ nonsurge deviation rejecting long trips that is profitable, there is a positive cutoff $2$ surge deviation rejecting short trips that is profitable, and the resulting measured dynamic reward functional is not dynamically incentive compatible.
items.review_theorem4_structural_policy_repres: For nonatomic two-state measures, arrival rates, affine price parameters, switch rates, and Theorem 4 bracket source data, there exists an optimal policy $\rho^\ast$ for the structured dynamic surge-price reward whose state-$0$ component is almost-everywhere equal to a nonsurge structural representative and whose state-$1$ component is almost-everywhere equal to a surge structural representative; every optimal policy admits such representatives in both states.
items.review_theorem3_feasible_sequential_curr: For measures $\mu$, arrivals, $\rho,R_1,R_2$, and switch rates $q_{12},q_{21}$, the Theorem 3 feasible sequential current-bounds source-data assumptions imply the measured structured IC and almost-everywhere uniqueness conclusion $\mathrm{theorem3MeasuredStructuredMeasurableICAEUniqueConclusion}(\mu,\mathrm{arrival},R_1,R_2,q_{12},q_{21})$.
